name: U(2) horizontal flavor symmetry
summary: Approximate U(2) symmetry acting on the first two fermion generations, with the third generation a singlet. Naturally produces the observed fermion-mass hierarchy (m_t >> m_c >> m_u) and the small mixing of the third generation with the lighter ones (V_ub, V_cb << 1) without a fully hierarchical Froggatt-Nielsen charge structure. In SUSY contexts, also suppresses flavor-changing neutral currents from squark mixing (the SUSY flavor problem).

addresses_puzzles.note: U(2) breaking pattern naturally produces 3rd-2nd hierarchy m_2/m_3 ~ epsilon, and 2nd-1st via small additional breaking m_1/m_2 ~ epsilon'. Less constraining than full FN but more 'minimal' for the dominant hierarchy.
V_cb ~ epsilon, V_ub ~ epsilon * epsilon' follow from U(2) breaking; reproduces Wolfenstein-like organisation.
U(2) horizontal symmetry between the first two families reduces the number of independent Yukawa parameters via spurion-controlled breaking (Pomarol-Tommasini 1995).

touches_benchmarks.note: U(2)-protected SUSY squark mixing suppresses K-Kbar and D-Dbar mixing relative to anarchic SUSY; fits observed bounds.
Predicts the lambda-hierarchical CKM organisation through U(2) breaking spurions.

new_symmetries: U(2) horizontal acting on first two generations
introduces.note: U(2) breaking pattern (small parameter epsilon ~ 0.05 for second-third generation, epsilon' ~ 0.005 for first-second) and Lambda_F.

history: Barbieri, Hall, Raby (1995); Barbieri-Hall, 'Squark and slepton mass relations from grand unified flavor symmetries' (Nucl. Phys. B 449, 437 (1995)). Pomarol and Tommasini (1996), 'Horizontal symmetries for the supersymmetric flavor problem', Nucl. Phys. B 466, 3, arXiv:hep-ph/9507462. Modern U(2)^3 extensions (Barbieri-Buttazzo-Sala-Straub 2011) for B-anomaly model-building.

references.citation: Pomarol and Tommasini, 'Horizontal symmetries for the supersymmetric flavor problem', Nucl. Phys. B 466, 3 (1996)
Barbieri, Buttazzo, Sala, Straub, 'Flavour physics from an approximate U(2)^3 symmetry', JHEP 07, 181 (2012)
